result.summary: Lucid exposes one authenticated REST API, a public MCP server, and the public `lucid-package` CLI; no GraphQL surface found.

lucid_api_key.label: Lucid API key
lucid_api_key.setup: Go to the [Lucid developer docs](https://developer.lucid.co/reference/creating-a-key) and create an API key for your account. Use the key as the REST API credential; Lucid notes that API keys are tied to your account and should be kept secure.

lucid_oauth_client.label: Lucid OAuth 2.0 client
lucid_oauth_client.setup: Register an OAuth 2.0 client in the [Lucid developer docs](https://developer.lucid.co/docs/oauth-20-client-creation). Lucid uses this client for OAuth-based access to its REST API; after creating the client, follow the REST API OAuth flow to obtain access tokens.
